title: Listening - Part 3: People They Are Close To
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a person they are close to. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the quality each speaker admires in that person. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a person they are close to. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the quality each speaker admires in that person. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people describe a person they are close to.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: My aunt lives across town and has a demanding job, so I know she isn't sitting at home with nothing to do. When I was preparing university applications, I was completely confused by all the forms and deadlines. [19]She came round every Sunday for a month, reading my personal statement and helping me organise everything, even though it took up most of her afternoon.[/19] I really appreciate that she never made me feel I was inconveniencing her.||Speaker 2 Man: My closest friend and her family had to leave their flat after a fire in the building. Almost all her clothes and books were damaged, and for weeks they stayed with relatives. I expected her to be miserable. [20]Instead, she kept saying how lucky they were that nobody was hurt, and she focused on planning their fresh start rather than complaining.[/20] Her attitude made me realise how strong she is when life becomes difficult.||Speaker 3 Woman: My brother and I have very different tastes, especially in music. Last year I performed with a band at college and everyone afterwards said it had been brilliant. He didn't. [21]He kindly told me that the opening song hadn't worked and explained exactly why, even though he knew I might be disappointed.[/21] I watched the recording later and realised he was right. I trust him because his compliments actually mean something.||Speaker 4 Man: I met my friend Daniel at my first job. We don't share many hobbies, but whenever something is bothering me, he's the person I call. [22]He never interrupts or immediately turns the conversation into a story about himself; he waits until I've explained everything and only offers advice if I ask for it.[/22] I usually understand my own problem better after speaking to him, simply because he's given me the space to think aloud.||Speaker 5 Woman: My grandfather and I are quite unlike each other in some ways. He loves gardening, while I can barely keep a house plant alive. Yet we always enjoy being together. [23]We both notice the absurd side of ordinary events, and a remark that nobody else in the family finds amusing can have the two of us laughing for ages.[/23] Family dinners are never dull when we're seated together, although everyone else sometimes wonders what we've found so funny.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: My aunt lives across town and has a demanding job, so I know she isn't sitting at home with nothing to do. When I was preparing university applications, I was completely confused by all the forms and deadlines. She came round every Sunday for a month, reading my personal statement and helping me organise everything, even though it took up most of her afternoon. I really appreciate that she never made me feel I was inconveniencing her.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: My closest friend and her family had to leave their flat after a fire in the building. Almost all her clothes and books were damaged, and for weeks they stayed with relatives. I expected her to be miserable. Instead, she kept saying how lucky they were that nobody was hurt, and she focused on planning their fresh start rather than complaining. Her attitude made me realise how strong she is when life becomes difficult.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My brother and I have very different tastes, especially in music. Last year I performed with a band at college and everyone afterwards said it had been brilliant. He didn't. He kindly told me that the opening song hadn't worked and explained exactly why, even though he knew I might be disappointed. I watched the recording later and realised he was right. I trust him because his compliments actually mean something.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: I met my friend Daniel at my first job. We don't share many hobbies, but whenever something is bothering me, he's the person I call. He never interrupts or immediately turns the conversation into a story about himself; he waits until I've explained everything and only offers advice if I ask for it. I usually understand my own problem better after speaking to him, simply because he's given me the space to think aloud.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: My grandfather and I are quite unlike each other in some ways. He loves gardening, while I can barely keep a house plant alive. Yet we always enjoy being together. We both notice the absurd side of ordinary events, and a remark that nobody else in the family finds amusing can have the two of us laughing for ages. Family dinners are never dull when we're seated together, although everyone else sometimes wonders what we've found so funny.

options.text: has remained positive in a difficult situation
is good at making people laugh
has been generous with their time
shares the speaker's sense of humour
forgives people when they make mistakes

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker values the amount of time her aunt freely gave to help with applications.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The friend stayed optimistic despite losing possessions and her home temporarily.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker trusts his brother because he gives truthful feedback rather than automatic praise.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Daniel is admired for listening patiently and attentively.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker and her grandfather enjoy the same kind of humour.
